request.question:
Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK will enable fermentation of xylose to ethanol at a yield of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions, compared to zero ethanol production in the parental strain, due to the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway.
In plain English: can we engineer baker's yeast to convert a sugar found in agricultural waste into fuel, something it cannot naturally do?

prior_feedback.note: Plasmid Extraction should be divided into multiple steps and account for 3hrs

hypothesis.original_hypothesis:
Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK will enable fermentation of xylose to ethanol at a yield of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions, compared to zero ethanol production in the parental strain, due to the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway.
In plain English: can we engineer baker's yeast to convert a sugar found in agricultural waste into fuel, something it cannot naturally do?

references.title: Comparison of the xylose reductase-xylitol dehydrogenase and the xylose isomerase pathways for xylose fermentation by recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses the xylose isomerase pathway in recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae, which is directly relevant to the proposed intervention and biological system.

references.title: Directed Evolution of Xylose Isomerase for Improved Xylose Catabolism and Fermentation in the Yeast Saccharomyces cerevisiae
references.relevance_note: This paper focuses on the directed evolution of xylose isomerase for improved fermentation in Saccharomyces cerevisiae, aligning with the experimental type and intervention.

plan.objective: To evaluate the ethanol yield from a Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK strain expressing heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. compared to the parental strain.
plan.experimental_design: The experiment will involve transforming Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK with a plasmid encoding the xylose isomerase gene. Fermentation will be conducted in controlled bioreactors with xylose as the sole carbon source. Ethanol yields will be measured and compared between the engineered strain and the parental strain over a period of 72 hours.
controls: Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK strain transformed with a plasmid containing the xylose isomerase gene from Piromyces sp. and grown in xylose medium.
Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK parental strain grown in xylose medium without the xylose isomerase gene.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 16 h | Plasmid Preparation
Grow 5 mL of E. coli containing the xylose isomerase plasmid in LB medium with 100 µg/mL ampicillin at 37°C for 16 h. Centrifuge at 4,000 × g for 10 min at 4°C and discard supernatant.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, 37°C incubator
Reagents: 5 mL LB medium, 100 µg/mL ampicillin
Expected outcome: E. coli culture reaches an OD600 of approximately 1.0.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 3 h | Plasmid Extraction
Use the Qiagen Plasmid Mini Kit to extract plasmid DNA from the E. coli pellet. Follow the protocol to resuspend the pellet in 50 µL Buffer P1, lyse with 50 µL Buffer P2 for 5 min, and neutralize with 50 µL Buffer N3. Centrifuge at 20,000 × g for 10 min at room temperature. Transfer supernatant to a new tube and add 250 µL Buffer PE. Centrifuge at 20,000 × g for 30 s and discard the flow-through. Elute plasmid DNA in 50 µL Buffer EB.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, Qiagen Plasmid Mini Kit
Expected outcome: Plasmid DNA concentration is ≥ 100 ng/µL.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 2 h | Transformation
Transform 50 µL of competent Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK cells with 10 µL of the extracted plasmid DNA using the lithium acetate method. Incubate at 30°C for 30 min, heat shock at 42°C for 15 min, and recover in 1 mL YPD medium for 1 h at 30°C. Plate 100 µL on YPD agar plates with 100 µg/mL G418 and incubate at 30°C for 48 h.
Equipment: 30°C incubator, YPD agar plates
Expected outcome: At least 50 transformants appear on the agar plates.

DAY 2 | 16 h | Colony Screening
Pick 5 colonies from the YPD agar plates and inoculate each into 5 mL YPD medium with 100 µg/mL G418. Incubate at 30°C for 16 h. Measure OD600 and extract plasmid DNA from each culture using the Qiagen Plasmid Mini Kit as described previously.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, 30°C incubator
Expected outcome: OD600 reaches approximately 1.0 for all cultures.

DAY 2 | 2 h | Plasmid Verification
Perform restriction enzyme digestion of 5 µg of plasmid DNA from each transformant using EcoRI and HindIII at 37°C for 1 h. Analyze the digested products on a 1% agarose gel at 100 V for 30 min.
Equipment: Agarose gel electrophoresis system, UV transilluminator
Expected outcome: Correct band sizes corresponding to the xylose isomerase insert are observed.

DAY 3 | 2 h | Fermentation Setup
Prepare 1 L of fermentation medium containing 50 g/L xylose, 5 g/L yeast extract, and 5 g/L peptone. Adjust pH to 5.5 with 1 M NaOH. Sterilize by autoclaving at 121°C for 15 min. Inoculate with 10% (v/v) of the transformed Saccharomyces cerevisiae culture and the parental strain into separate bioreactors.
Equipment: Autoclave, 1 L bioreactors
Reagents: 50 g/L xylose, 5 g/L yeast extract, 5 g/L peptone
Expected outcome: Medium is sterile and pH is adjusted to 5.5.

DAY 3 | 72 h | Fermentation
Set bioreactors to 30°C with stirring at 200 rpm and continuous CO2 venting. Monitor fermentation for 72 h, measuring ethanol concentration every 12 h using gas chromatography (GC).
Equipment: Bioreactor, Gas chromatograph (Agilent 7890B)
Expected outcome: Ethanol concentration is measured at each time point.

DAY 4 | 1 h | Ethanol Quantification
Collect 1 mL samples from each bioreactor every 12 h. Dilute samples 1:10 in distilled water and analyze using gas chromatography with a flame ionization detector at 250°C. Use a standard curve of known ethanol concentrations for quantification.
Equipment: Gas chromatograph (Agilent 7890B)
Expected outcome: Ethanol concentrations are quantified with a standard curve correlation coefficient (R²) ≥ 0.99.

DAY 4 | 1 h | Data Compilation
Compile ethanol yield data from the fermentation process for both the engineered and parental strains. Calculate ethanol yield as grams of ethanol produced per gram of xylose consumed.
Expected outcome: Ethanol yield data is compiled for analysis.

DAY 4 | 6 h | Statistical Analysis
Conduct statistical analysis using GraphPad Prism 9.0. Perform a two-tailed t-test to compare ethanol yields between the engineered and parental strains. Set significance level at p < 0.05.
Equipment: GraphPad Prism 9.0
Expected outcome: Statistical significance is determined with p-values reported.

DAY 5 | 2 h | Results Interpretation
Interpret the statistical results and compare the ethanol yields of the engineered strain against the threshold of 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose. Document findings and prepare for presentation.
Expected outcome: Ethanol yield from the engineered strain is documented and compared to the threshold.

DAY 5 | 3 h | Documentation
Compile all experimental data, methods, and results into a comprehensive report. Include figures and tables summarizing the findings and statistical analysis.
Expected outcome: Final report is prepared for review.

DAY 5 | 30 min | Plasmid Storage
Store the extracted plasmid DNA at -20°C for future use. Aliquot into 50 µL portions in sterile microcentrifuge tubes.
Equipment: -20°C freezer, Microcentrifuge tubes
Expected outcome: Plasmid DNA is stored securely for future experiments.

DAY 5 | 1 h | Bioreactor Cleaning
Clean bioreactors thoroughly with 70% ethanol and rinse with distilled water. Autoclave before next use.
Equipment: Autoclave
Expected outcome: Bioreactors are cleaned and sterilized for future experiments.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: The xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. will function effectively in Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK.
Ethanol production will be measurable and quantifiable using gas chromatography.
The fermentation conditions will support growth and metabolism of the engineered strain.
decision_criteria: Proceed if ethanol yield from the engineered strain is ≥ 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose.
Proceed if statistical analysis shows p < 0.05 for the difference in yields between strains.
Proceed if at least 3 independent replicates yield consistent results.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of xylose for all fermentations.
Standardize the inoculum size to 10% (v/v) for all cultures.
Calibrate the gas chromatograph before each analysis to ensure accurate measurements.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.
